data.title: NetApp provides conservative guidance, analysts note improving margins
data.description:
NetApp (NASDAQ:NTAP) shares dipped after the enterprise storage and data management company provided tighter revenue guidance than the market expected in its latest earnings, but analysts noted margins for fiscal 2026 were higher than anticipated.

"F26 revenue guidance was $6.75bn at the mid-point, which is below Street expectations of $6.84bn," said Bank of America Securities analysts, led by Wamsi Mohan, in an investor note. "Gross margin & operating margin guidance of 71.5% & 29.3% at the mid-point came in above Street expectations of 70.9% & 28.3%. The margins are impacted early in F26 by longer than expected weakness in product GMs but offset by Public Cloud GMs at the high-end of the 75-80% l-t range as well as better mix."

The previously announced divestiture of Spot to Flexera for about $100M will also improve NetApp's gross margins further in its Public Cloud segment, Mohan noted.

BofA reiterated its Neutral rating and nudged its price target to $121 from $120.

"We suspect there is a dose of conservatism in the guide as well given this is the 1st guide from the new CFO (Wissam Jabre)," according to Evercore ISI analysts, led by Amit Daryanani, in a note. "From a product perspective, All Flash array continues to drive Hybrid Cloud, growing 14% to reach an ARR of $4.1B, representing ~66% of the Hybrid Cloud segment."

"While NTAP faces a more dynamic macro-environment, we think the company is being conservative with their guide and that there could be upside from enterprise AI deployments, stability on government spending, and a better margin/commodity environment," Evercore said. "Specifically, NTAP noted sizable AI deals with large enterprises that are expected to close later this year."

NetApp indicated that artificial intelligence inferencing presents a greater opportunity than model training.

"We are helping customers deploy AI inferencing in production today and expect fiscal year 2026 to be a pivotal year for enterprise AI storage, with the opportunity outstripping that of model training," said NetApp CEO George Kurian, during Thursday's earnings call.

Flash competitor Pure Storage (PSTG) was relatively flat during early Friday trading. Dell Technologies (DELL) and Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) had both slipped 1%.

data.title: Electronic Arts points to strong Marvel relationship despite the Black Panther game cancellation
data.description:
Electronic Arts (NASDAQ:EA) said its decision to pull the plug on its Black Panther game and close the studio making is not indicative of a major change with the Marvel partnership. The company confirmed this week that it still has at least three additional Marvel games in development.

EA Entertainment president Laura Miele said the decision to cancel the Black Panther game would help the publisher sharpen its focus and put creative energy behind the most significant growth opportunities. Miele also noted that EA Motive's Iron Man game was unaffected

An Electronic Arts (NASDAQ:EA) spokesperson characterized the multi-title, long-term relationship between Marvel Games and EA as strong. The first game in the partnership is expected to be the aforementioned single-player, third-person, action-adventure Iron Man title, currently in development at Motive Studio in Montreal. Both companies describe the collaboration as a creative partnership, with Marvel providing the freedom and support for EA’s studios to craft original stories and gameplay experiences.

Marvel has been owned by Walt Disney Company since 2009.

data.title: Four Corners Property Trust acquires properties worth $9.6M
data.description:
Retail REIT Four Corners Property Trust (NYSE:FCPT) announced on Thursday the acquisition via sale-leaseback of a Christian Brothers Automotive property for $4.3M.

The Illinois-based property is under a long-term lease guaranteed by the corporate entity.

FCPT said the transaction was priced at a cap rate in range with the company's previous transactions.

Separately, the REIT announced the acquisition of an automotive service property leased to a national operator for $5.3M.

The Arkansas-based property is under a corporate long-term triple net lease.

The transaction was priced at a 6.8% cap rate on rent as of the closing date and exclusive of transaction costs.

data.title: GDP growth annualized in Canada increased to 2.2% in Q1
data.description:
• GDP Growth Annualized in Canada increased to 2.2% in the first quarter of 2025 from 2.10% in the fourth quarter of 2024.

  • The Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in Canada expanded 0.5% in the first quarter of 2025 over the previous quarter.

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWC), (NYSEARCA:FLCA).
  • Currency: (USD:CAD) (USD:CAD)

data.title: Stock index futures fall as inflation gauge comes in-line
data.description:
Stock index futures were little changed on Friday as investors awaited any more news related to President Trump's tariffs, while the inflation gauge came in-line

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.6%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.6%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.44%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.96%.

The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit on Thursday issued an administrative stay blocking a ruling from a lower court on Wednesday that struck down “reciprocal” tariffs Donald Trump imposed in April.

So even if the court ruling is upheld, it doesn’t look like the world is heading back to the pre-2025 tariff situation, even if there is a pathway to a world with less tariffs after the ruling, Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The Fed's preferred inflation gauge rose 0.1% in April, as expected.

Wall Street's major averages on Thursday ended with marginal gains amid a bond rally sparked by weak GDP data and more back-and-forth on tariffs.

“Risk assets initially surged yesterday after the surprise court ruling, but they gave up those gains later in the day as markets digested several alternative avenues they could pursue,” Reid said.

The U.S. trade deficit narrowed in April.

The May consumer sentiment and the May Chicago PMI will land during market hours. The former is expected to come in at 50.8 and the latter at 45.1.

Final May Michigan consumer sentiment data says little of itself. However, comparisons to the preliminary data suggest the evolution of partisan bias as Trump retreated from trade taxes, Donovan added.
